headline: Data gaps dominate; extreme net-short COT is the lone bullish spark
summary: Critical feeds are largely offline: live front/next prices, storage, weather, and fair-value all returned null, leaving only seasonality and COT positioning as actionable inputs. On those two signals the bias is mildly bullish — early cooling season (day 21/121) adds slight demand tailwind, and managed-money net positioning at the 3rd percentile of its 5-year range is a historically strong contrarian long setup. However, the COT trend is still moving more short (-7,763 W/W as of 9 Jun), suggesting capitulation has not yet occurred, and acting without confirmed price and storage data carries unacceptable execution risk. Prior close on front was ~$3.198; monitoring for a confirmed reversal catalyst before initiating.

position.rationale: Insufficient live price and fundamental data to define a reliable entry/stop/target with confidence.

drivers: MM net positioning at 3rd percentile (5-yr): -122,617 contracts — historically contrarian bullish setup
Early cooling season (day 21 of 121) provides mild AC-demand tailwind
Live price feed returned null; only prior close (~$3.198 front) available — stale for active trading
Storage, weather short/long-term, and fair-value signals all offline, leaving the picture severely incomplete
COT long/short ratio of 0.61x confirms skewed short positioning, but trend still worsening W/W
risks: Short positioning could extend further before any reversal, deepening losses on a premature long
If summer cooling demand disappoints (mild weather), the seasonal tailwind evaporates quickly
Data restoration may reveal bearish storage surplus or weak fair-value that overrides the COT signal
disclaimer: Informational only, not financial advice. Auto-generated hourly.
